# scrapedoctl

Scrape.do CLI & MCP Server. Multi-provider web search and scraping for AI agents with anti-bot bypassing and persistent caching.

## MCP Tools

- **`scrape_url`**: Scrapes any URL to optimized Markdown.
  - `url`: target URL (required)
  - `render`: enable JS rendering (optional)
  - `super`: use residential proxies (optional)
  - `geoCode`: target country (optional)
  - `actions`: browser actions like clicks/scrolling (optional)

- **`web_search`**: Search the web using multiple engines and providers.
  - `query`: search query (required)
  - `engine`: google, bing, yandex, duckduckgo, baidu, yahoo, naver (optional)
  - `provider`: scrapedo, scraperapi, serpapi (optional)
  - `lang`: language code (optional)
  - `country`: country code (optional)
  - `limit`: max results (optional)

## CLI Commands

- `scrapedoctl search <query>`: Multi-engine web search with table/JSON/Markdown output.
- `scrapedoctl scrape <url>`: Scrape a URL to Markdown.
- `scrapedoctl account`: Show usage, limits, and credits for all configured providers (Scrape.do, SerpAPI, ScraperAPI). Supports `--json` for machine-readable output.
- `scrapedoctl usage`: Local usage analytics from SQLite. Shows requests by provider/action. Flags: `--week`, `--month`, `--all`, `--json`.
- `scrapedoctl repl`: Cisco-style interactive shell with prefix matching and context help (?). Includes `show account` and `show usage` subcommands.
- `scrapedoctl install`: Interactive setup for AI agents (Claude, Gemini, Codex, Kimi, etc.).
- `scrapedoctl install init`: Non-interactive setup that generates .mcp.json, CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, GEMINI.md project files.
- `scrapedoctl version`: Show version and check for updates.
- `scrapedoctl update`: Check for new releases with install instructions.
- `scrapedoctl completion install <shell>`: Install shell completions (bash/zsh/fish).
- `scrapedoctl metadata`: JSON representation of the tool.

## Search Providers

- Scrape.do: Google (structured JSON via /plugin/google/search)
- ScraperAPI: Google (structured JSON via /structured/google/search)
- SerpAPI: Google, Bing, Yandex, DuckDuckGo, Baidu, Yahoo, Naver
- Exec plugins: Custom providers via stdin/stdout JSON protocol
